conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can assess your situation accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility rules. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you'd like to claim Child Benefit for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have a child. My son Alex, he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

Are you or your partner (if you have one) earning over £60,000 per year? This won't affect your eligibility, but it's important information as there may be a tax charge to consider.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why you qualify:
- Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training)
- You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for him
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (only one person can claim per child)

Additionally, since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you won't need to worry about the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

You can now proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. If you need help with the application process, please let me know!

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for him under the eligibility rules. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is required as only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. Based on these factors, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key criteria: they are responsible for a child under 16, they live with the child in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for the same child. Neither the claimant nor their partner earns over £60,000, so there will be no High Income Child Benefit tax charge to consider.
